rulings.Case Title: THEDA FUNG VS. SUSANA MANCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order; Declaration Of John S. Rueppel In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THEDA FUNG'S Motion For A Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for protective order is granted. Daher has not met his burden of justifying that 212 special interrogatories are warranted, as required under CCP 2030.040. Daher may propound up to 35 interrogatories to which Plaintiff will provide Code-compliant responses. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF TAD DEVLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff Devlin's motion for summary judgment on his complaint for breach of the settlement agreement is DENIED. There are triable issues of material fact regarding the existence and/or terms of a settlement. (See Devlin Dec., par. 6, Ex. A; Eubank Dec., pars. 6-8, Ex. 4; Eubank Dispute of Declaration of Tad Devlin, 2:13-4:6.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Ms. Eubank is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Cornelia Wong, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS SERGEY GRECHKO, MAYYA GRECHKO, LUDMILA GRECHKO, OLGA GRECHKO, NICHOLAS GRECHKO, NICHOLAS GRECHKO, ALEX GRECHKO'S Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Cornelia Wong, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Most of Defendant's responses do not comply with CCP 2031.210-2031.250. Within 30 days, Defendant shall serve Code-compliant responses and any additional responsive documents, along with a privilege log for any information withheld on privilege grounds. Defendant shall also provide a verification that conforms with CCP 2015.5 signed by Ms. Wong. Sanctions are granted. Ms. Wong shall deliver a check payable to Sergey Grechko in the amount of $1,320 to Volkov Law Office within 30 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories General, Set One, To Cornelia Wong, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS SERGEY GRECHKO, MAYYA GRECHKO, LUDMILA GRECHKO, OLGA GRECHKO, NICHOLAS GRECHKO, NICHOLAS GRECHKO, ALEX GRECHKO'S Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories General, Set One, To Cornelia Wong, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. CCP 2030.250(a) requires verification by the responding party. Defendant shall provide a verification that conforms with CCP 2015.5 signed by Cornelia Wong within 10 days. The request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's motion to strike is DENIED for the same reasons its jointly filed demurrer is overruled. Also, the complaint adequately pleads grounds for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: DAVID QUIJANO VS. GROCERY OUTLET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT J.J. ZHU CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant J.J. Zhu Corporation's demurrer is OVERRULED. Notwithstanding the workers' compensation regime, employers may be sued for assaults by their employees under authorization and/or ratification theories; the complaint adequately so pleads. (See, e.g., Iverson v. Atlas Pacific Engineering (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 219, 228-29; Herrick v. Quality Hotels, Inns & Resorts, Inc. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 1608 1618.) Further, defendant raises disputes of fact not appropriate for a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BELINDA H. ET AL VS. ANTHEM BLUE CROSS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTS ANTHEM BLUE CROSS AND REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. As plaintiffs effectively concede they should be, defendants' demurrers are SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Several of the issues raised should have been resolved in the meet-and- confer required by Code of Civil Procedure §430.41. If an amended complaint is filed, counsel are urged to meet and confer in good faith before filing any more demurrers. (See id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CRAIG P RAMSDELL VS. ALI A AALAEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Entire Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 24, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANTS ALI AALAEI AND ARI LAW, P.C.'S Motion To Seal Entire Case. Defendants' motion to seal "entire case" is DENIED. The motion fails to show that sealing the "entire case" is necessary to further the identified overriding interest/s. The motion therefore does not comply with the narrowly tailored requirement of CRC 2.550(d)(4) and (5). The court is obligated to follow the constraints of the CRC notwithstanding the parties' stipulation to seal the entire case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
